name: Hero modal

variants.name: single_step
variants.description: One acknowledging action. e.g. Got it, Continue, I understand.

variants.name: multi_step
variants.description: Multiple steps with Previous / Next navigation. Final step has a confirming primary action.

headline.note: Short and specific, focus on core feature value

body.note: Text paragraph or custom structured layout (consuming team responsible)

hero_content.note: Image or animated content. May differ between light/dark mode. Consuming team implements.

steps.note: Total number of steps for multi-step flow

margin_from_screen_edge.note: Minimum distance to top and bottom of screen

overlay.note: Semitransparent overlay covers the full underlying screen

usage_rules.detail: Hero modals interrupt the user's workflow. Only use to introduce new features or explain functionality. Do not use for routine confirmations.
Hero modal does not have a fullscreen variant. Do not attempt to make it fullscreen.
When open, the semitransparent overlay prevents interaction with underlying content.

usage_rules.rule: Hero area and actions stay fixed on scroll
usage_rules.detail: If content overflows, only the headline and body scroll. Hero image and action buttons remain fixed.
The modal is always centered on screen. Do not position it to one side.
In multi-step flows, use Next and Previous for navigation. Final step must end with a clear confirming action.
Got it, Continue, I understand, Enable, Try out are all acceptable action labels.

confidence_blockers: If ticket does not provide hero_content specification, flag — consuming team must implement.
If ticket requests fullscreen hero modal, flag as not supported.

embedding_text: Hero modal component. Introduces new features onboarding announcements. Has hero image area and body content area. Single step with acknowledge action or multi-step with next previous navigation. Centered on screen with overlay. Sparingly use only for feature introduction. No fullscreen. Hero and actions fixed on scroll.
